title: saussurea alpina subsp. depressa
intro: Saussurea alpina subsp. depressa, also known as the Himalayan or White Rhubarb, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and possessing antioxidant properties. Saussurea alpina subsp. depressa is often prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or extract, and can also be used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
